metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_267
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Regulation of Ca++ stores in spines.
And in turn regulation of morphological and functional spine plasticity.
"The mechanism through which SP may promote plasticity is indicated by the observations that STIM-1, the sensor of calcium concentration in stores, and Orai-1, the calcium-induced calcium entry channel, are colocalized with SP, in the same dendritic spines."
Comparison of WT and SP-KO mice.
Figure 6. Ca++ transients in response to photolysis of caged glutamate are larger in synaptopodin-containing spines regardless of spine shape

17/3/2017 Pim
Fig.1/2: SP-KO results in changed synaptic transmission
Fig.6: Ca++ transients in response to photolysis of caged glutamate are larger in synaptopodin-containing spines regardless of spine shape
Fig.7: intracellular Ca-stores are responsible
Fig.8/9: SER is involved since STIM1 and Orai1 move into spines.. Experimental description: Mice were obtained from a colony at the University of Freiburg (Deller et al., 2003), bred and raised at a local animal facility.
